Defendants Ge Healthcare Inc. And General Electric Company'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer; Supporting Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-22-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adam M. Rapp, Esq. of Glynn & Finley LLP, T:925.210.2803, for defendant/ moving party;
Jeremy Moseley, Esq. of Wheeler Trigg O'Donnell T:303.244.1843, for defendant/ moving party;
Lawrence D. Goldberg, Esq. of Bowles & Verna LLP, T:925.935.3300, for plaintiff/ opposing party.

RULING: The matter was discussed. Counsel for moving party does not disagree with the tentative ruling but requested that the motion be denied without prejudice. After hearing, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS GE HEALTHCARE, INC. AND GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY'S Motion To Transfer, modified as follows:.

Defendants GE Healthcare Inc. And General Electric Company's motion to transfer venue to Riverside County Superior Court pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 397(c) is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Defendants have submitted no supporting declarations or affidavits and have failed to meet their burden to show that transfer is warranted to promote the convenience of witness and the ends of justice. (Lieppman v. Lieber (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 914, 919 ["'The burden rests upon one who seeks a change of venue under [Code of Civil Procedure, section 397, subdivision 3] to prove that both the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice will be promoted thereby, and this he must do through affidavits that contain more than generalities and conclusions.'"]; Stute v. Burinda (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d Supp. 11, 17 ["When a motion for change of venue is made under Code of Civil Procedure, section 397, subdivision 3, on the grounds that the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the changes, the supporting declarations must show (1) the names of each witness, (2) the expected testimony of each witness, and (3) facts showing why the attendance of said witnesses at trial will be inconvenient and why the ends of justice would be served by a transfer"]); Flanagan v. Flanagan (1959) 175 Cal.App.2d 641, 645-646 [the affidavit must aver facts from which the court to make a finding as to the convenience of witness and to conclude that the end of justice would be served by the venue change].) Defendants' sole reliance on plaintiffs' allegations in the unverified complaint is misplaced. "Such a motion must, of course, be supported by competent evidence." (Tutor-Saliba-Perini Joint Venture v. Superior Court (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 736, 744.)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Aug-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adam M. Rapp, Esq. of Glynn & Finley LLP, T:925.210.2803, for defendant/ moving party;
Jeremy Moseley, Esq. of Wheeler Trigg O'Donnell T:303.244.1843, for defendant/ moving party;
Lawrence D. Goldberg, Esq. of Bowles & Verna LLP, T:925.935.3300, for plaintiff/ opposing party.

RULING: No party discussed or objected to the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS GE HEALTHCARE, INC. AND GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Therefore, the tentative ruling was adopted as follows:

The application of Michael L. O'Donnell to appear pro hac vice as counsel to Defendants GE Healthcare Inc. and General Electric Company is granted. No opposition filed, and the application conforms to the requirements of Cal. R. Ct. 9.40.

The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Aug-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS GE HEALTHCARE, INC. AND GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

No appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The verified application of Jeremy A. Moseley to appear as counsel pro hac vice on behalf of Defendants GE Healthcare Inc. and General Electric Company is granted. No opposition filed, and the application conforms to Cal. R. Ct. 9.40.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)
